steps.title: Horizontal line tangent to parabola means it passes through the vertex
steps.content:
A horizontal line intersects a parabola at exactly one point when it is tangent to the parabola—that is, when it touches the parabola at the vertex.

For the parabola $y = -2x^2 + 11x$ with $a = -2 \lt 0$, the vertex is the maximum point.

The line $2y = c$ (equivalently, $y = \frac{c}{2}$) must pass through this vertex.

steps.title: Find the x-coordinate of the vertex
steps.content:
Using the vertex formula with $a = -2$ and $b = 11$:
$$x = -\frac{11}{2(-2)} = \frac{11}{4}$$

steps.title: Find the y-coordinate of the vertex and calculate c
steps.content:
Substitute $x = \frac{11}{4}$ into $y = -2x^2 + 11x$:
$$y = -2\left(\frac{11}{4}\right)^2 + 11\left(\frac{11}{4}\right) = -2 \cdot \frac{121}{16} + \frac{121}{4}$$
$$= -\frac{121}{8} + \frac{121}{4} = -\frac{121}{8} + \frac{242}{8} = \frac{121}{8}$$
Since the line $2y = c$ passes through the vertex where $y = \frac{121}{8}$:
$$c = 2 \cdot \frac{121}{8} = \frac{121}{4} = 30.25$$
